title: dactylis glomerata subsp. hylodes
intro: Dactylis glomerata subsp. hylodes, commonly known as orchard grass, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Dactylis glomerata subsp. hylodes can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
